matches.title: Frontend Developer
matches.reasoning: Your proficiency in HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, along with a keen eye for styling and design, makes Frontend development a natural match. You enjoy building visual interfaces and coding up responsive interactions, matching your preference for building user-facing systems.

tasks: Master ES6+ features (Promises, Async/Await, Destructuring, Modules).
Learn TailwindCSS and responsive web design practices.
Build a responsive landing page using Tailwind and vanilla JS animations.
Learn React components, props, state, and hooks (useState, useEffect).
Understand rendering cycles and listing keys.
Create a dynamic dashboard like a weather app or task manager.
Learn Single Page Application routing (React Router).
Understand global state management (Zustand or Redux Toolkit).
Integrate third-party REST APIs into a React application using Fetch or Axios.
Learn TypeScript types, interfaces, and generics in a React context.
Understand unit testing basics using Jest and React Testing Library.
Convert an existing JS project to TS.
Learn optimization techniques (lazy loading, code splitting, memoization).
Get familiar with Server-Side Rendering (SSR) using Next.js.
Build a small SEO-optimized portfolio blog on Next.js.
Optimize and host 3 primary projects on Vercel or Netlify.
Optimize Google Lighthouse scores for your portfolio site.
Practice web development interview questions (system design, web security, accessibility).

matches.title: UI/UX Designer
matches.reasoning: You have a strong passion for layout, typography, and design aesthetics. Combining this with your coding knowledge allows you to communicate incredibly well with engineers, which is a rare superpower in UI/UX teams.

tasks: Study typography hierarchy, grid systems, and color theory.
Master Figma Auto-Layout, Components, and Variables.
Design a responsive desktop and mobile landing page concept.
Learn user interview techniques and create user personas.
Map out user flows and design site maps.
Conduct a heuristic evaluation of an existing product and propose redesign wireframes.
Learn smart-animate and micro-interactions in Figma.
Create high-fidelity interactive mockups for a mobile application.
Conduct usability tests on your prototype and document findings.
Build a reusable UI library in Figma (buttons, inputs, cards).
Learn how to write clear design specs for developers.
Collaborate on a mock handoff workflow.

matches.title: Product Designer
matches.reasoning: Product Designers look at both business objectives and user needs. Given your interest in design and technical viability (knowing how code works), you can bridge user experience and commercial success.

tasks: Learn product discovery techniques and value proposition mapping.
Study how UI changes impact key business metrics (conversion, retention).
Analyze a popular product's user onboarding funnel.
Learn motion design principles for UI.
Build complex, interactive application flows using Figma or ProtoPie.
Run competitive analysis on UI patterns of leading apps.
Study growth loops, referral systems, and premium paywall layouts.
Design a growth feature experiment dashboard.
Draft a portfolio case study highlighting business impact of your design choices.
